name: GRAFT-VERSUS-HOST DISEASE
definition: T lymphocytes from donor bone marrow cause selected epithelial damage of recipient target

demographics.Frequency: 30-70% of patients with allogeneic (= donor genetically different from host)
demographics.Histo: centrilobular hemorrhagic necrosis, venular obliteration, sinusoidal congestion,
demographics.Location: distal ileum > other small bowel > colon

dominant_finding: moderate bowel wall thickening < 5.5-8.0 mm: small bowel involved in 75-100%
distribution: distal ileum > other small bowel > colon
discriminating_features: moderate bowel wall thickening < 5.5-8.0 mm: small bowel involved in 75-100%
fluid-filled distended bowel: colon > 8 cm, small bowel > 3 cm
barium (from prior contrast enema weeks ago) may become incorporated into bowel

CT: moderate bowel wall thickening < 5.5-8.0 mm: small bowel involved in 75-100%
fluid-filled distended bowel: colon > 8 cm, small bowel > 3 cm
barium (from prior contrast enema weeks ago) may become incorporated into bowel
misty mesentery = mesenteric fat stranding without lymphadenopathy
CECT: abnormally enhancing thin layer of mucosa diffusely involving thickened bowel
Skin: thickening of gallbladder wall ± pericholecystic fluid
enhancement of wall of gallbladder and CBD
GI tract: separation of bowel loops ← wall thickening
"ribbon bowel" = small bowel fold effacement with tubular appearance (DDx: viral
"toothpaste / leadpipe / moulage" sign = featureless segment of bowel (chronic
loss of haustration, spasm, edema, ulceration, granular mucosal pattern of colon
small bowel "cast" HIGHLY SUGGESTIVE = prolonged coating of abnormal bowel for
circular collections of contrast material on cross section + parallel tracks on

oral contrast material should not be given!
clinical.Cx: infection with opportunistic organisms, eg, Candida albicans, herpes virus, invasive fungal
clinical.Prognosis: fatal in up to 15% ← opportunistic infection
